mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. The key trade-off is that it runs entirely in Docker, which may not reflect all aspects of a production environment.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the pre-built binary for your platform.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-darwin-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

[ $(uname -m) = arm64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-darwin-arm64 && chmod +x ./kind && mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 Linux
> For ARM64 Linux
> For Intel Macs
> For M1 / ARM Macs

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> This command creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0.

steps.title: Set Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up the kubeconfig file to interact with the Kind cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path --name=

```

> This exports the kubeconfig path for the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the cluster are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

uninstall.title: Remove the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in the cluster will be lost.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of any important data. This step upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

kind delete cluster

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Reinstall Kind with the latest version.
> Delete the existing cluster if needed.
> Recreate the cluster with the desired Kubernetes version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kind is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure Kind is installed and the binary is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start nodes: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker may not be running or there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure you have enough resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster creation is taking too long, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check Docker resource limits and increase them if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to get node ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The nodes are not in a 'Ready' state.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the nodes using 'kubectl logs <node-name>' to diagnose.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31.0, improved error messages during cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.resources: Minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM recommended for Docker.
prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
